claim: Population-scale community structure: first quantification of arm-level Leiden partition across HPRCv2.
metric: 41 arms → 15 communities; 233 individuals × 465 haplotypes × 15,668 PHR sequences; silhouette 0.347; modularity 0.97.

claim: Three-category arm classification (homogeneous / polymorphic / fully interchangeable); quantitative extension of Mefford & Trask's qualitative patchwork model.
metric: Homogeneous 8/41 (0% cross-arm); polymorphic 34/41 (≥2 seq-level communities); fully interchangeable 7/41 (100% cross-arm).

claim: Population-scale cross-arm affinity, first across 465 haplotypes.
Subtelomeric type discordance at population scale.
metric: Up to 47.5% structural heterozygosity per locus; chr15_p discordance 27.3% (6/22 individuals).

claim: Gene-repertoire replacement scores: first homogenisation gradient mapped onto community structure.
metric: Complete (0.91–1.00) at chr13_p / chr14_p / chr15_p / PAR; partial (0.0–0.72) autosomal communities; chr14_p (N=229) 83.0% cross-arm with other acrocentric p-arms.

claim: 3D genome mirrors sequence communities across three independent technologies.
metric: Hi-C / Pore-C B/W 0.027–0.074 (8 datasets); RPE-1 HPRC B/W 0.005–0.052 (3 datasets); Dip-C T2T 6.9% closer (16 cells, p=2.4×10⁻⁵, Mantel ρ=0.30); 50-community partition does not reach significance in Dip-C.

claim: Flanking-region paradox — strong evidence against multi-mapping artefact.
metric: HG002 100 kb flanking B/W = 0.002 vs PHR B/W = 0.027 (13× stronger flanking signal); flanking ρ strengthens away from tip, ruling out Rabl alone.

claim: C4 minimal-PHR positive control.
metric: 5–25 kb at chr7_q / chr12_q tips → 3D co-localisation in 4/5 diploid Hi-C samples; zero gene annotations.

claim: Cell-type specificity (PBMC vs LCL).
metric: PBMC W/B = 0.983, p = 0.305 (n.s.; mixed cells, hg19 projection, smaller PHRs); GM12878 T2T 6.9% closer (primary Dip-C finding).

claim: Per-individual discordance ↔ 3D correlation.
metric: Low discordance → strong Hi-C signal; ρ = −0.50, p = 3.4 × 10⁻⁴, N = 48 sample × community pairs.

claim: Proposed feedback-loop model: similarity → 3D proximity → ectopic exchange → increased similarity.
metric: Schematic; 3 of 4 links supported by present data; causal direction not established.

claim: MTCO pseudogene enrichment at acrocentric p-arms (C7); enabled by T2T-quality assemblies.
metric: Population-level enrichment in C7; per-community gene-class composition.

claim: TAR1 near-absence from PAR1.
metric: 0.5% at chrX_p / chrY_p vs 94.8% genome-wide; near-universal outside PAR (99.6%).

claim: Weak A-compartment with interior radial positioning.
metric: 68% A-compartment but mean e1 = +0.007; Dip-C radial 0.50–0.63.

claim: Subtelomeric exchange frequencies as phylogenetic markers — out-of-Africa topology recovered.
metric: 9 arm/community × 5 superpopulations recover AFR-deepest split (0.73–0.81), then EAS, then SAS/AMR/EUR (AMR–EUR 0.22).

claim: TAR1 prevalence consistent with passenger status (not a facilitator).
metric: C7 cross-arm rates 86.6% (TAR1+) vs 90.5% (TAR1−); near-universal prevalence outside PAR (99.6%) precludes a definitive functional test.

claim: Internal (TTAGGG)n islands at population scale (distinct from TAR1 / terminal arrays).
metric: 18,352 islands / 8,321 sequences (53.1%) / all 41 arms; median 79 bp.

claim: Alleles closer than paralogs except at C7.
metric: Wilcoxon p < 1×10⁻³⁰⁰ in 8/9 multi-arm communities; C7 reversed (paralog distance < allelic distance) in 65.5% of individuals.

claim: Subtelomeric Fst mirrors out-of-Africa history.
metric: AFR Fst 0.10–0.12 vs all non-AFR; non-AFR 0.00–0.02; mean Fst = 0.048 across 9 arms.

claim: Internal (TTAGGG)n islands predominantly degenerate; quantifies Ambrosini variant motifs.
metric: Only 32.2% pure canonical; TGAGGG 19.0%, TTGGGG 16.0%, TCAGGG 12.7%.

claim: Cross-arm and self-arm sequences share identical (TTAGGG)n island distribution.
Within-community Jaccard distance is multi-modal.
metric: C2/C12 bimodal (allelic vs paralog peaks); C1/C7 diffuse (homogenised).

claim: Two-domain subtelomeric model supported across most arms at pangenome scale.
metric: Gradient on 39/48 arms (81%); breakpoint on 39/41 testable arms (95%); 99.7% of haplotype sequences. Internal (TTAGGG)n co-localises with breakpoint within 25 kb on 11/19 testable arms.

claim: Two-domain boundary positions quantified at sequence resolution.
metric: Arm-specific 15–445 kb; chr4p 70 kb, chr4q 50 kb, chr22q 15 kb; consistent with FISH-inferred 25–50 kb but at sequence resolution.

claim: Mouse meiotic enrichment across all stages.
metric: Per-arm-pair ρ peaks at zygotene (ρ = 0.715, p = 4.4 × 10⁻⁵⁵, n = 344); leptotene 0.680, pachytene 0.677, diplotene 0.574; flanking ρ = 0.60–0.77 matches temporal pattern.

claim: Pangenome community-free framework validated cross-organism.
metric: Mouse 1 Mb per-arm-pair ρ 0.574–0.715; flanking 0.604–0.766; human Dip-C 16-cell Mantel ρ = 0.296, p = 0.002.

claim: Sperm signal in haploid post-meiotic cells.
metric: W/B = 0.401 (60% closer); Mantel ρ = 0.202, p = 0.023; consistent with Rabl persistence through spermiogenesis.
